gauge.name: Middle Island Creek near Middlebourne

impacts.statement: Homes on Stealy Street and Hadock Street near the creek flood.
Homes along Heritage Drive west of Middlebourne flood. The 4-H Dining Hall at the gauge floods.
Homes just above and below the gauge flood. The 4-H bridge at the gauge floods.
The 4-H building at the gauge and Bridgeway Road flood.
WV Route 18 at the gauge and much of the Tyler County Fairgrounds flood.
RV park at Tyler County Fairgrounds floods along with a portion of the fairgrounds itself.
Sections of WV Route 18 above the gauge flood.
County Route 24 just above Thomas Run floods.
Low and poor drainage areas along the creek and tributaries flood.

properties.caption: County Route 24 just above Thomas Run floods at 21 feet
RV Park at Tyler County Fairgrounds floods at 26 feet. View is of the creek at 23.5 feet.
Most of the Tyler County Fairgrounds floods at 27 feet. View is of the creek at 23.5 feet.
WV Route 18 at the gauge floods at 27 feet
Homes on Stealy Street near the creek flood at 31 feet
4-H building at the gauge floods at 28 feet and the 4-H dining hall floods at 30 feet

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are not available. Only observed stages are available for this point.
